title: galium hypotrichium var. tomentellum
intro: Galium hypotrichium var. tomentellum, also known as Woolly Bedstraw, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Woolly Bedstraw include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. Woolly Bedstraw can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies for its purported benefits.
